# Column drag to the locked-end region fails to re-home (silent)

## Context

Operator live report (2026-06-11, post-merge of PR #12881 + PR #12882, dev pulled, devindex reloaded): **"column DD still broken."** Reproduced within minutes on merged dev (`b190f60b9`) with a fresh-context native-input probe — the breakage is the **center → locked-end cross-region leg**, one of the deferred matrix cases (3–5) from #12878's characterization that no live test had covered post-fix.

## The Problem

Probe results on merged dev (fresh chromium per case, `page.mouse + {steps:60}`, 1920×1080):

| Case | Drag | Result |
|---|---|---|
| C3 center→start | `Total` → x=200 (inside start region) | ✅ re-homes: start `[#,Rank,User,Total]`, center 33→32 |
| C4 start→center | `User` → x=700 | ✅ re-homes: start 3→2, center 33→34, `User` at center idx 0 |
| **C5 center→end** | `Commits %` → x=1600 (inside end region 1543–1663) | ❌ **NO re-home**: end stays `[Updated]`, center count stays 33, `Commits %` merely slides right within center. Zero console/page errors — silent. |
| Sanity within-center | `Total` → x=585 | ✅ idx 2 (PR #12882 behavior holds) |

**The verification-doctrine point (operator-named, empirically proven tonight):** PR #12882's 11 unit specs were green while this leg was broken — units exercise the landing resolver in isolation, not the region pipeline (snapshot → positional verdict → `column.locked` mutation → `onColumnLockChange` redistribution → body sync). **Whitebox e2e through the live pipeline is the only evidence class that counts for this surface.**

## The Architectural Reality

- The C3/C4 successes prove the shared pipeline (drag-start region-rect snapshot in `draggable.grid.header.toolbar.SortZone#onDragStart` → `getDropRegion` → lock mutation → redistribution) works — the failure is **end-leg-specific**.
- Two branch hypotheses (`hypothesis — needs V-B-A before implementation`, in likelihood order):
  1. **`grid.Container#bodyEnd` unresolved at snapshot time** → snapshot `{end: null}` → `getDropRegion(1600, …)` skips the end check → verdict `null` = column's current `locked` → no change. `bodyEnd` is a nullable config (`src/grid/Container.mjs` ~L93); its population path vs `bodyStart`'s (~L809) needs the diff.
  2. **End-leg of `onColumnLockChange` redistribution fails silently** (start-leg verified working).
- Discriminator is cheap: log the snapshot rects at drag-start (one temporary line) or assert `gridContainer.bodyEnd` truthiness via the whitebox fixture before the drag.

## The Fix

1. Discriminate hypothesis 1 vs 2 via the whitebox path (NL fixture or worker-side assertion — NOT page-level only).
2. Fix the end leg.
3. **Regression coverage: a committed whitebox e2e spec** (per `.agents/skills/whitebox-e2e/`) covering the full matrix — within-region ×2, cross-region ×4 legs (center↔start, center↔end), landing indices AND membership AND body-cell sync. The probe recipe (boot pattern, drag helper, geometry, reference assertions) is documented on #12807 (comment `IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABFrw46Q`) and composes with @neo-opus-ada's simulatability lane there.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AC1: Hypothesis discriminated with evidence posted here (bodyEnd-at-snapshot vs end-redistribution)
- [ ] AC2: `Commits %` → x=1600 re-homes to the end region (end = 2 items, column.locked = 'end', body cells follow)
- [ ] AC3: Committed whitebox e2e spec covering the 6-case matrix above, green in CI — unit-only evidence explicitly insufficient for this surface (operator doctrine)
- [ ] AC4: C3/C4/within-region legs stay green (no regression of the working legs)

## Out of Scope

- The Neural Link window-registration enhancement (separate ticket, filed alongside this one) — the e2e fixture path works without it.
- Drag-feedback visual indicators (#12792's deferred polish — still unfiled, still the original author's lane).

## Decision Record impact

none.

## Related

Lineage: #12878 (corruption, fixed PR #12881) → #12880 (landing index, fixed PR #12882) → this (end-region pipeline leg). Verification substrate: #12807 (@neo-opus-ada). Umbrella: #9486. Operator report: 2026-06-11 ~01:42Z.

### @neo-fable - 2026-06-11T01:49:08Z

**Input from Claude Fable 5 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## Symptom refinement (operator, live): the broken layer is the IN-DRAG VISUAL CHOREOGRAPHY — even within-center
> 
> > "even inside the center region: headers MOVE to switch position with css transform. they no longer move to correct positions. not even close."
> 
> This reframes the ticket: **final landing order is correct** (all probes assert it), but the **mid-drag visual layer** — the non-dragged headers sliding to their swapped slots via `wrapperStyle.left` (`container.SortZone#updateItem` → absolute-positioned items) — lands at grossly wrong positions during the drag. Every verification layer so far was blind to it:
> 
> - PR #12882's unit harness **stubs `updateItem()`** — the visual write path is untested by design.
> - All live probes (the author's and mine) asserted **post-drop DOM order only**.
> - The pipeline can be "green" everywhere while looking completely broken to a human — the strongest case yet for the operator's whitebox-e2e doctrine: the spec must assert **mid-drag geometry**, not just end state.
> 
> ### Hypothesis set (all `hypothesis — needs V-B-A`, discriminable in one instrumented run)
> 
> 1. **Burst-walk reactive no-op:** #12882's while-loop now fires N `switchItems` per event (was max 1). `updateItem` mutates the SAME `wrapperStyle` object reference and reassigns it (`wrapperStyle.left = X; item.wrapperStyle = wrapperStyle`) — under a synchronous burst, reactive-config equality/batching may swallow intermediate (or all) writes, leaving headers wherever the last delivered write put them.
> 2. **Swap-math drift under bursts with unequal widths:** hand-verification of a 0→2 walk holds the slot-partition invariant, but live `Rectangle` instances (vs plain objects) + the grid subclass's parallel `columnPositions` mutations per step may diverge from the parent's rect bookkeeping mid-burst.
> 3. **Scroll-offset in the band mapping:** `getDragMoveTargetIndex` adds the ZONE's `scrollLeft` to a viewport-space `clientX` and compares against viewport-space snapshot rects — if the center region is horizontally scrolled when the drag starts (the operator's real session, unlike the probes' scrollLeft=0), every band lookup is offset by the scroll delta → switches fire for wrong slots → "not even close" both visually and (at scroll≠0) in landing. **Cheap discriminator: scroll the center region right ~300px, then drag — if landing also goes wrong, this is live.**
> 
> ### Falsifier recipe for the visual layer (extends the #12807 recipe)
> 
> Between dispatched mousemoves, capture `getBoundingClientRect()` of the first ~6 center headers and compare against the expected slot partition (snapshot widths re-partitioned per executed switch). One `page.evaluate` per move-step; the divergence trace IS AC1's evidence.
> 
> ### AC amendments (ticket author)
> 
> - **AC2 amended:** correct re-home AND correct mid-drag visual positions (headers occupy the expected slot partition within ±2px after each switch).
> - **AC3 amended:** the committed whitebox spec asserts mid-drag geometry at ≥2 checkpoints per drag, plus scrolled-region variants (scrollLeft ≠ 0) — final-order-only assertions are explicitly insufficient (tonight's empirical lesson, twice over).

> 
> `git diff origin/main..e0c15daf3 -- src/draggable/container/SortZone.mjs` (v12.1 → dev-just-before-PR-#12882) returns **EMPTY**. The parent motion engine — `onDragMove` cadence + threshold/hysteresis, `switchItems` swap math, `updateItem` wrapperStyle choreography, the overdrag auto-scroll path — was **byte-identical** between:
> 
> - v12.1 `main` (operator-verified working, single body), and
> - dev as of one week ago through last night (operator-verified working drag visuals WITH multi-body — i.e., the +268-line grid subclass and +224-line `header.Wrapper` multi-body work composed cleanly with the untouched v12.1 parent engine).
> 
> **Therefore:** the within-center motion regression ("headers move to wrong positions, not even close") and the broken overdrag sideways-scroll are pinned to **PR #12882's parent-class changes** — the in-bound early-return (which bypasses the original threshold path, its anti-jitter `moveFactor`, and changes the overdrag interplay) plus the burst-walk (N synchronous `switchItems`/`updateItem` cycles per event, a write pattern the reactive wrapperStyle path never had to absorb before).
> 
> **Revert calculus is now decisive, not judgment:** reverting PR #12882 restores a parent file byte-identical to BOTH known-good baselines. PR #12881 (grid subclass only) is untouched by it. > 
> Re-land path for #12880 stays: the landing-resolver concept survives as the TARGETING layer; the re-land must drive the EXISTING visual cadence (one switch per qualifying event, hysteresis preserved) rather than replacing it with a burst-walk, and must pass mid-drag geometry assertions through the #12886 eyes.

### @neo-fable - 2026-06-11T07:18:04Z

**Input from Claude Fable 5 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## Sighted-fix session 1 results: two legs FIXED (PR #12891), the third leg precisely localized
> 
> **Fixed + matrix-verified** (full mechanism narrative in PR #12891): duplicate drag:move delivery (in-bound same-position guard, overdrag exempt) + the viewport-vs-owner-relative coordinate mismatch (THE locked-only mechanism — delta inflated by the toolbar's viewport origin: ~388px locked-center vs ~17px unlocked, explaining the operator's datum exactly) + the `NeoArray` import (end-region lock-changes resurrected: the unlock op that threw `ReferenceError` now succeeds live).
> 
> **The remaining leg — cross-toolbar DOM move (the duplication/loss class), first real catch by `verify_component_consistency`:**
> 
> Case 5 repro (drag `Commits %` center→end, drop x=1600, post-import-fix): the lock-change re-home now executes — center 33→32 ✓ — but:
> 
> ```
> toolbar-3 (end):  items: [button-2, button-37]   ✓ engine correct
>                   vdom:  [button-2, button-37]   ✓ vdom correct
>                   DOM:   [button-37]             ✗ rendered DOM never applied the move
> ```
> 
> The engine and vdom both re-home the column; **the vdom→DOM patch for a componentId node moving BETWEEN toolbar trees doesn't apply** (and the inverse interleaving — old parent keeps the node while the new parent renders it — is the operator's observed drop-DUPLICATION). Suspect surface: the redistribution's per-toolbar update flow (two sibling containers updating independently in one cycle; a cross-parent node move needs either a single combined update at the common ancestor — `header.Wrapper` — or an explicit `moveNode` delta, the pattern `createDragProxy` already uses manually).
> 
> **Next session's pickup:** trace the redistribution update path in `grid/header/Wrapper.mjs` + `Container#onColumnLockChange`; fix via combined-ancestor update or moveNode delta; verify with the consistency probe (it makes the bug binary) + cases 5/6 + the duplication interleaving. All rig recipes + this evidence are on-ticket.

### @neo-fable - 2026-06-11T07:33:11Z

**Input from Claude Fable 5 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## Operator manual-test refinement (post-#12891 dev, locked devindex): the remaining visual-layer legs, precisely three
> 
> > "dragproxy no longer contains the content of the column. the dragproxy movements are fine. however, when i drag one column to the right, OTHER columns slide to the left (like tab-resorting in chrome), and this part does not work yet (dom rects at wrong positions). only tried devindex with the locked-columns version though."
> 
> **Leg A — proxy content missing:** the drag proxy follows the cursor correctly but renders EMPTY — the `moveColumnContent` surgical DOM move (`createDragProxy`'s `moveNode` deltas transplanting live cell content into the proxy) silently fails in the locked grid. Suspects: the per-region `gridBody` resolution feeding `getColumnCells`/`columnPositions`, or the moveNode deltas targeting ids that the multi-body layout renamed/relocated. The proxy-restore path in `onDragEnd` shares the surface.
> 
> **Leg B — neighbor-slide choreography at WRONG positions:** the Chrome-tab-style slide of non-dragged headers lands at wrong DOM rects (operator) / no movement at all (my synthetic-dispatch `observe_motion` trace — flat through 92 samples while switches fired). Prime hypothesis now: **containing-block mismatch** — `updateItem` writes `wrapperStyle.left` in TOOLBAR-relative coordinates (via `adjustProxyRectToParent`), which renders correctly only if the owner toolbar establishes the absolute-positioning containing block; in the multi-toolbar `header.Wrapper` layout the containing block may be the Wrapper/container → toolbar-relative lefts render offset by the toolbar's own origin (~388px for center) = "wrong positions", small-or-zero error in single-toolbar grids = locked-only, consistent with every datum including #12891's coordinate-space conviction (same space-mismatch family, render side). Falsifier: `observe_motion` during a real-pointer drag + `get_computed_styles` (position/left + the containing block's position) on a sliding neighbor mid-drag.
> 
> **Leg C — cross-toolbar DOM move** (banked above): engine+vdom re-home, DOM doesn't apply — `verify_component_consistency` binary.
> 
> All three are render-layer; the decision layer is fully fixed (#12891). Next block: falsify B's containing-block first (one computed-styles call), then A's delta targets, then C's redistribution flow — likely one PR, verified by `observe_motion` showing the slide land at the expected slot partition + the consistency probe staying green + the operator's eyes on the proxy content.

- 2026-06-11T09:27:35Z @neo-opus-ada cross-referenced by PR #12893
### @neo-fable - 2026-06-11T09:54:48Z

Reopening: operator's post-merge L4 manual pass caught a residual — header cells and body cells of SLIDING columns desync during multi-switch drags. Two further mechanisms convicted live and fixed on `agent/12883-render-layer` (follow-up PR incoming):

1. **Mid-drag mapping staleness (my own regression from the region-local fix):** `switchItems` moves `columnPositions` per switch, so from the second switch on, the index-math reverse map resolved cells one-off — drag-hide/slide writes landed on the wrong columns. Fix: `getCellId`/`getDataField` now resolve through the MATERIALIZED slot binding (`data.field` stamped by `Row#createVdom`), which cannot drift; index math remains only as the pre-first-render fallback.

2. **Chromium `moveBefore()` stale flex layout (observed Chromium 146 + 149):** after the drop's `moveNode` delta, the toolbar's DOM order was correct while the rendered flex order stayed stale — one neighbor kept painting at its pre-move slot, surviving structural child-list changes; only a box rebuild heals it. Deterministic single-call repro (`moveBefore(button-2, before button-3)` → DOM `[1,2,3,4]`, rendered `[2,3,1,4]`). Fix: `DeltaUpdates.moveNode` rebuilds the parent's boxes synchronously after `moveBefore` (no paint in between; iframes/canvas survive display toggles — only CSS-animation continuity resets), with focus preservation mirroring the legacy fallback.
